path: PBS Digital Innovator Program Overview Webinar_12.12.18.pdf
text:
# Page 1^page=1
PBS Digital Innovators: The Future is Here!

# Page 2^page=2
● Where We Are and How We Got Here ○ Program Evolution ○ Strengths and Weaknesses ○ Current PBS Educator Recognition Programs ● Where We Are Heading ○ Vision for the Future ○ Models for Participation ○ What Makes a Good Candidate ● How We Will Get There ○ Resources ○ Checklists/Things to Consider ○ Timelines ● Questions Agenda

# Page 3^page=3
Program Evolution 2013 Small Group of Product Ambassadors Nominated by local PBS station, advancement of digital learning in education. 2014 Large Group of Product Ambassadors Selected by application, focus on tech-savvy teachers incorporating digital media and technology in the classroom. 2015 30 “Lead” Digital Innovators Summit attached to ISTE. 100 local DI’s with “30” leads. 2016 Lead DI from Every State Recognized excellence in using technology and digital media to support student learning, 100 local DI’s 52 “leads” that attended the summit. 2017 52 DI’s from Every State and Territory Emphasis on classroom changemakers. 2018 All-Stars Selected by Local DI’s

# Page 4^page=4
Strengths and Weaknesses Online community encourages ongoing growth and sharing Application process brings great educators, but sometimes our stations can’t work with them Tech focus can be limiting to the applicant pool Building national educator community, but not cultivating state Reciprocal relationship between PBS, Stations and Educators Program can put star educators onto PBS/Station “engagement ladder”

# Page 5^page=5
PBS Recognition Program: PBS Digital Innovator All Stars ● Every other year station/state/regional programs will nominate DI’s to become an All-Star ● Selected All-Stars and station reps will attend the All-Star summit (tied to ISTE) ● Two year program: first year virtual professional learning series, developed with stations; second year deeper engagement with station and PBS

# Page 6^page=6
PBS Recognition Program: Early Learning Champs ● Recognize, spotlight and celebrate educators of young children ● Facilitate pathways for professional growth ● Develop strong relationships between local educators, stations and stakeholders ● Create community and networking opportunities for educators of young children ● Lift up and elevate voices of early childhood educators

# Page 7^page=7
PBS Recognition Program: Early Learning Champs ● Every other year station/states/regions who have an established Early Learning engagement strategy in their community will nominate Early Learning Champions ● Selected Champions and station reps will attend the Early Learning Champion summit (tied to NAEYC) ● Two year program: first year personal growth, second year elevate their learning (all while building and establishing communities of practice) ● Will run another program in 2019

# Page 8^page=8
Future Vision: State + ‘Digital Innovator’ = #Winning! Localized programs (with PBS Support), paired with an exclusive “All Star” experience Recognized educators will lead and support stations with local initiatives All Stars will provide national exposure and platforms, while helping to grow online teacher communities

# Page 9^page=9
Models Station Recognition Program ● Each station runs and own their own “teacher recognition” program ● Each station will then have their own group of teachers working on station specific programs ● If station already has a teacher recognition program, that program can be leveraged State Recognition Program ● Stations across a particular state can partner together and have a state-wide DI program ● Not all stations in one state have to participate in a statewide program ● This allows educators in states with some stations who are not participating to have a “home” Regional Recognition Program ● Stations in each region can partner together and have a regional DI program ● This helps to build relationships between states and stations ● A good rural solution ● Helps distribute capacity needs

# Page 10^page=10
Who and What are Classroom Changemakers Who ● Educators who are “classroom changemakers” ● Educators who may help local stations with education events and products ● Educators who are we can learn from, and who can learn from us ● PBS Nerds What ● Could be focused on one area of practice (all science teachers) ● Could already be a program under a name other than “digital innovator” ● Professional growth tracks could be created and innovated by All Stars ● Could hold state/station/regional summits

# Page 11^page=11
What Resources Will Be Available? ● Program Guidebook ● Marketing and Outreach Supports ● Application Support for local programs ● Selection Process Guidelines and Program Framework for both All Star and Champions Programs ● Clear Timeline ● Eventually: State/Station/Region Summits

# Page 12^page=12
Building a Program? ❏ Make some decisions: which model, team with others, let us know, combo Digital Innovators and Champions?, budget ❏ Participate in upcoming webinars ❏ Join our Recognition Program basecamp grow ❏ Use our Guide (Available Feb 4, 2019) ❏ Best Practices ❏ Effective Outreach and Engagement Methods ❏ Social Media Toolkit ❏ Digital Assets ❏ PR Guidance ❏ Marketing emails and advertising ❏ Application Support ❏ Selection Process Guidelines ❏ Program Timeline ❏ Eventually: State/Station/Region Summits

# Page 13^page=13
Already Have a Program? ❏ Make some decisions: use application support, change name, partner with others? ❏ Join our Recognition Program basecamp group: perhaps offer to be a station mentor ❏ Review our All Star and Champion selection Process Guidelines ❏ Align Program Timeline ❏ Interest in forming your own community of practice? Let us know ❏ Eventually: State/Station/Region Summits

# Page 14^page=14
Proposed High Level Timeline 2018 - 2019 DEC JAN FEB MAR APR MAY JUN JUL Intro webinar - application support available Support materials available Recommended timeframe to recruit and select local teachers Local program work with teachers AUG SEP OCT

# Page 15^page=15
Proposed All-Star Timeline NOV 2019 - 2020 DEC JAN FEB MAR APR MAY JUN JUL AUG SEP 2019 All-Star selection process All-Star Summit All-Stars selected Summit prep Engage with All-Stars OCT Local selection process

# Page 16^page=16
Thoughts? Questions?
